threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the train' and ask locals about 'getting to Zones.'
The 196 bus often has a troupe of late-night street performers on board. Last week, they did an impromptu dance-off that had even the driver laughing.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the guy selling homemade Döner from his bike while students debated philosophy next to a mural of Rammstein. Pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a club in a former brewery that's also a dog park by day. Barking beats techno any time!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to avoid Civic Center during BART drama. They’ve seen the chaos from a blown brake or a stray dog too many times.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 late last night, driver stopped to let a raccoon cross. Only in the Tenderloin do Muni detours include wildlife.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume all apartments have the classic Catalan 'català' charm, but many locals opt for stark minimalist decor. It's a whole vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still struggling with the 'u' in 'Barcelona'—saying it like a soft 'b' instead of a hard 'b' like it's written.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you ask for directions to a bus stop and everyone looks at you like you've just landed from Mars.
Saw a guy last night on the 333 break dancing in the aisle. No one batted an eye; I guess that’s just Brixton after dark.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: It hit me when I saw a group of kids playing Fußball in the street while a protest marched by, and nobody batted an eye. Total Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do they close down streets for a party to celebrate a sausage maker’s anniversary. You can smell the bratwurst from blocks away!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local when they instinctively redirect foot traffic around a street performer without missing a step, all while scrolling their phone.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 bus today with a guy passionately debating the merits of sourdough. Muni's like a moving coffee shop sometimes.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think all the good tapas are in El Born; meanwhile, the best patatas bravas are hiding in a corner bar on Carrer de Fraternitat.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment while ordering a simple bocadillo—it's the look of someone expecting a sandwich shop to be straightforward.
